title: WELD: The Provable Raffle
tagline: A raffle nobody can rig: tickets are unforgeable, the draw is freshness-proven, and any browser recomputes the same winner.
holy_shit: No operator, no server, no trust: tickets are committed to a future drand beacon round so the issuer can't know the outcome (the-unriggable-ticket), and the draw is seeded with the SHA-256 of live public bytes your browser fetched right now — a cryptographic freshness proof proving the result couldn't have been known in advance and can't be backdated (proof-of-freshness). Anyone reruns the exact draw in their own browser and gets the same winner. A fair lottery with no house.
powered_by: Unriggable-ticket oracle cell (verify-before-exec drand beacon resolution) welded to proof-of-freshness byte-hash: draw entropy = SHA-256(freshness_hash ':' round_randomness), winner_index = that entropy mod n_tickets.
weld: The draw seed is the SHA-256 of live public bytes (freshness proof) combined with a future drand round's randomness, so the winner is unriggable, provably can't be known before the draw, can't be backdated, and only valid committed tickets are eligible.
agent_scenario: When you need a raffle where tickets are unforgeable and committed before the draw, the draw entropy is provably fresh (SHA-256 of live public bytes) and unriggable (future drand randomness), and any browser reruns the same deterministic selection — no trusted operator needed.
author: kody-w + Copilot
